projects.title: Projects
projects.desc: Data tools, telemetry systems and performance engineering.

circuit-simulation.title: Interactive Circuit Simulation
circuit-simulation.description: Real-time circuit simulation with curvature-based speed control
circuit-simulation.pageDesc: Path interpolation + Canvas API. A car that follows the track based on real curvature data.

sections.title: Context
sections.body: Started as a way to represent an F1 circuit visually — not a static image, but something with real movement and physics.

sections.title: Problem
sections.body: Make the car follow the track smoothly, with speed that varies based on actual corner geometry — not just a fixed rate.

sections.title: Solution
sections.body: A system of centerline points with distance-based interpolation. Speed is computed from local curvature: tight corners slow the car, straights speed it up.

sections.title: Real-world reference
sections.body: Lap time is calibrated to the actual circuit record — 1:14.637, Michael Schumacher, Ferrari, 2006.

sections.title: What I learned
sections.body: Canvas animation loops, geometry for trajectory systems, and real-time rendering performance in the browser.

fastf1-analysis.title: F1 Race Analysis
fastf1-analysis.description: Real telemetry data analysis using FastF1 — lap times, tyre strategy and pit stop detection from official F1 API data.
fastf1-analysis.pageDesc: Real telemetry data analysis using FastF1 — lap times, pit stop detection and tyre strategy from official F1 API data.

sections.title: Context
sections.body: A self-directed learning project towards F1 software engineering. Using real race data to understand how teams analyze strategy, tyre degradation and driver performance.

sections.title: What it does
sections.body: Compares two drivers lap-by-lap with automatic pit stop detection, tyre compound labels and interactive tooltips. Analysis of Piastri vs Antonelli at the 2026 Miami GP revealed Antonelli's undercut attempt on lap 26 vs Piastri's lap 28 stop.

sections.title: Real-world reference
sections.body: Data sourced directly from the official F1 timing API via FastF1 — the same data feed used by teams during race weekends.

sections.title: What I learned
sections.body: How to work with real telemetry data, detect pit stops programmatically from lap time deltas, and build clear visualizations that tell a story from raw numbers.
